description: Nutanix VMA -- Kubernetes operator to migrate Nutanix AHV VMs to KubeVirt. Cold migration (power off, snapshot, export, CDI import, create VM) and experimental warm migration (CBT-based incremental sync). Custom Prism API client, mock Nutanix server for testing, pre-flight validation, hooks, kubectl plugin, observability.

userStories.title: Project Scaffolding
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Go module, kubebuilder project, Makefile, Dockerfile, GitHub Actions CI, LICENSE (Apache 2.0), README, and AGENTS.md initialized so that subsequent stories have a buildable project foundation. Install kubebuilder if not present. Run: kubebuilder init --domain nutanix.io --repo github.com/nctiggy/nutanix-vma --project-name nutanix-vma. Entry point is cmd/main.go (kubebuilder v4 default). Add kubevirt.io/api and kubevirt.io/containerized-data-importer-api to go.mod. Extend kubebuilder Makefile with test-integration, test-e2e, docker-push targets. Dockerfile: multi-stage, gcr.io/distroless/static:nonroot base. AGENTS.md: include build commands, project structure, testing conventions, key design decisions (see PRD Section 6). CI workflow: .github/workflows/ci.yaml on push/PR runs make lint, make build, make test. Go 1.22+. golangci-lint config.
acceptanceCriteria: go build ./... succeeds
make lint passes
make test runs (exits 0)
GitHub Actions CI workflow triggers on push
Apache 2.0 LICENSE file present
README.md with project description and build instructions
AGENTS.md with Ralph instructions (build commands, project structure, conventions)
Dockerfile builds successfully
userStories.notes: kubebuilder generates ~20 scaffolding files automatically. The 5-8 file limit applies to hand-written files only. kubebuilder v4 places entry point at cmd/main.go, NOT cmd/operator/main.go.

userStories.title: CRD Types -- Provider, NetworkMap, StorageMap
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the core CRD types defined so that the operator has a declarative API. Define in api/v1alpha1/: NutanixProvider (spec: URL, SecretRef, RefreshInterval, InsecureSkipVerify; status: Phase, VMCount, Conditions), NetworkMap (spec: ProviderRef, Map []NetworkPair with Source ID/Name and Destination Type pod/multus, Name, Namespace), StorageMap (spec: ProviderRef, Map []StoragePair with Source ID/Name and Destination StorageClass, VolumeMode, AccessMode). Create groupversion_info.go for vma.nutanix.io/v1alpha1. Create sample CRs in config/samples/.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 CRD types defined with kubebuilder markers
make generate && make manifests succeeds
CRD YAML generated in config/crd/bases/
Sample CRs in config/samples/
make build && make test passes
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 1. Do NOT use kubebuilder create api (it generates controller stubs we don't want yet). Hand-write the type files.

userStories.title: CRD Types -- MigrationPlan, Migration, Hook
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the migration CRD types defined. MigrationPlan (spec: ProviderRef, TargetNamespace, Type cold/warm, NetworkMapRef, StorageMapRef, VMs []PlanVM, MaxInFlight, TargetPowerState, WarmConfig; status: Phase, VMs []VMValidationStatus with Concerns). Migration (spec: PlanRef, Cutover timestamp, Cancel []string; status: Phase, Started, Completed, VMs []VMMigrationStatus). VMMigrationStatus MUST include resource tracking fields for idempotency: SnapshotUUID, ImageUUIDs []string, TaskUUID, DataVolumeNames []string, OriginalPowerState. Hook (spec: Image, Playbook base64, Deadline, ServiceAccount; status: Conditions). Add sample CRs.
acceptanceCriteria: 3 CRD types defined with kubebuilder markers
VMMigrationStatus includes resource tracking fields (SnapshotUUID, ImageUUIDs, TaskUUID, DataVolumeNames, OriginalPowerState)
make generate && make manifests succeeds, adding to existing CRD output
Sample CRs for Plan, Migration, Hook in config/samples/
make build && make test passes
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 2a. Resource tracking fields are critical for idempotency in Story 13a -- do not omit them.

userStories.title: Nutanix API Client -- Core + Auth
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the NutanixClient interface and HTTP client foundation. Create internal/nutanix/types.go with forward-declared minimal structs (VM, Disk, NIC, BootConfig, GPU, Reference, IPAddress, Subnet, StorageContainer, RecoveryPoint, Image, Task, CBTClusterInfo, ChangedRegions, ChangedRegion, PowerState). Create client.go with NutanixClient interface (all 20 methods: ListVMs, GetVM, PowerOffVM, PowerOnVM, GetVMPowerState, CreateRecoveryPoint, GetRecoveryPoint, DeleteRecoveryPoint, CreateImageFromDisk, GetImage, DownloadImage, DeleteImage, CloneVMFromRecoveryPoint, DeleteVM, DiscoverClusterForCBT, GetChangedRegions, ListSubnets, ListStorageContainers, ListClusters), ClientConfig, NewClient(), httpClient with stubs returning errors.New('not implemented: MethodName'). Create auth.go (Basic Auth, custom TLS, InsecureSkipVerify). Create tasks.go (pollTask helper for GET /api/prism/v4.0/config/tasks/{uuid}). Implement retry with exponential backoff on 429/5xx. Define NutanixAPIError type.
acceptanceCriteria: NutanixClient interface fully defined with all 20 methods
Forward-declared types allow interface to compile
NewClient(config) returns working client
Basic Auth header set on all requests
Custom CA and InsecureSkipVerify work
Task polling helper polls until SUCCEEDED/FAILED
Retry logic with exponential backoff on 429/5xx
All unimplemented methods return clear not-implemented errors
Unit tests with httptest for auth, retry, task polling
make build passes with no compilation errors
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 1. Does NOT depend on CRD stories. Forward-declared types are minimal -- full struct fields added in Story 4.

userStories.title: Nutanix API Client -- VM Operations
userStories.description: As a developer, I need VM CRUD and power operations. Populate full struct fields in internal/nutanix/types.go from PRD Section 6 'Nutanix API Response Types' (VM, Disk, NIC, BootConfig, GPU, Reference, IPAddress, Subnet, StorageContainer, RecoveryPoint, Image, Task, CBTClusterInfo, ChangedRegions, ChangedRegion). Create vm.go: ListVMs (paginated via $top/$skip, v4 API), GetVM, PowerOffVM (async task -> poll), PowerOnVM (async task -> poll), GetVMPowerState. Replace stubs in client.go.
acceptanceCriteria: VM list with pagination support (handles >500 VMs)
VM get returns full spec (disks, NICs, boot config, cluster reference)
Power on/off uses async task polling until complete
Go structs match Nutanix v4 API schema from PRD Section 6
Unit tests for each method with httptest mock endpoints
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 3. v4 pagination uses metadata.totalAvailableResults and metadata.offset.

userStories.title: Nutanix API Client -- Snapshots & Images
userStories.description: As a developer, I need snapshot and image operations. Create snapshot.go: CreateRecoveryPoint (POST -> task -> poll), GetRecoveryPoint, DeleteRecoveryPoint. Create image.go: CreateImageFromDisk (POST with data_source_reference -> task -> poll), GetImage, DownloadImage (stream to io.Writer, NOT buffer in memory), DeleteImage. Also implement CloneVMFromRecoveryPoint fallback path. Replace stubs in client.go.
acceptanceCriteria: Recovery point create/get/delete with async task polling
Image creation from vDisk reference (primary path)
CloneVMFromRecoveryPoint + image from clone + cleanup (fallback path)
Image download streams to io.Writer without buffering full image
Unit tests for each method including fallback path

userStories.title: Nutanix API Client -- Networking, Storage & Cluster Discovery
userStories.description: As a developer, I need networking, storage, and cluster discovery operations. Create network.go: ListSubnets (v4 networking API on PC). Create storage.go: ListStorageContainers (v2 API, takes PE URL as param). Create cluster.go: ListClusters (v4 clustermgmt API, returns cluster IPs for PE auto-discovery). Replace stubs in client.go.
acceptanceCriteria: Subnet list returns UUID, name, VLAN ID
Storage container list works via Prism Element URL
Cluster list returns PE IPs for auto-discovery
Unit tests for each method with httptest

userStories.title: Nutanix API Client -- CBT/CRT
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Changed Block Tracking operations for warm migration. Create cbt.go: DiscoverClusterForCBT (POST to PC, returns CBTClusterInfo with PE URL + JWT token), GetChangedRegions (GET to PE with JWT in cookie NTNX_IGW_SESSION, paginated with nextOffset, up to 10000 regions per page). JWT refresh: if token age > 12 minutes, re-discover to get new token. Replace stubs in client.go.
acceptanceCriteria: Two-step flow: PC discovery returns PE URL + JWT token
Changed regions query with offset/length/block_size params
JWT token passed in cookie header (NTNX_IGW_SESSION)
Pagination via nextOffset until nil
Token refresh when nearing 15-min expiry
Zero-region identification in response
Unit tests: single page, multi-page, token refresh, zero regions
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 5 and Story 6. This is for warm migration (experimental) but the client code is needed by the mock server.

userStories.title: Mock Nutanix API Server -- Core
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a mock Nutanix API server for testing. Create pkg/mock/store.go (in-memory state: VMs, subnets, containers, clusters, tasks -- thread-safe with sync.RWMutex). Create pkg/mock/server.go (NewServer() returns *httptest.Server, WithFixtures() loads default data: 3 VMs, 2 subnets, 1 container, 1 cluster). Create handler files: handlers_vm.go (list paginated, get, power-off -> task), handlers_network.go (list subnets), handlers_storage.go (list containers), handlers_task.go (QUEUED->RUNNING->SUCCEEDED after 2 polls), handlers_cluster.go (list clusters).
acceptanceCriteria: In-process test helper with mock.NewServer()
VM list/get/power-off endpoints return correct data
Subnet and storage container list endpoints work
Task polling simulation (state transitions after polls)
Default fixture data loads with WithFixtures()
Tests verify all endpoints return expected responses
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 4 and Story 6 (needs nutanix types for response structures). At the 8-file limit -- keep handlers_network and handlers_storage small.

userStories.title: Mock Nutanix API Server -- Snapshots, Images, CBT
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the mock server to support snapshot, image, and CBT operations. Add handlers_snapshot.go (create -> task, get, delete -> task), handlers_image.go (create -> task, get, download with synthetic data + Range header support, delete), handlers_cbt.go (cluster discovery returning PE URL + JWT, changed regions with pagination). Create cmd/mock-nutanix/main.go as standalone binary with --port flag. Update server.go to register new routes and store.go for new state.
acceptanceCriteria: Snapshot CRUD via mock with async tasks
Image create/download with configurable size and HTTP Range header support
CBT two-step flow with JWT token validation
CBT pagination with configurable changed regions
Standalone binary: go run cmd/mock-nutanix/main.go --port 9440
Tests verify snapshot/image/CBT flows

userStories.title: VM Builder -- Metadata Translation
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to translate Nutanix VM metadata to KubeVirt VirtualMachine specs. Create internal/builder/vm.go: Build(vm, networkMap, storageMap, opts) returns *kubevirtv1.VirtualMachine. Create internal/builder/sanitize.go: SanitizeName() (lowercase, replace spaces/underscores/parens with hyphens, collapse, trim, truncate 63 chars, append -N on collision). CPU: preserve topology (sockets->sockets, cores->cores, threads->threads default 1). Memory: bytes->MiB. Firmware: LEGACY->bios, UEFI->efi, SECURE_BOOT->efi+secureBoot. Disks: DISK only (skip CDROM without error), SCSI/PCI->bus:scsi, IDE/SATA->bus:sata, bootOrder:1 on boot disk. NICs: pod->masquerade, multus->bridge+MAC preserved. Machine: Q35->q35, PC->pc. Labels on metadata AND template.metadata.
acceptanceCriteria: CPU topology preserved (sockets/cores/threads, NOT flattened)
All firmware types mapped correctly (LEGACY/UEFI/SECURE_BOOT)
CDROM entries skipped without error
IDE disks mapped to sata bus
MAC address preserved on bridge interfaces, not on masquerade
Machine type uses KubeVirt aliases (q35, pc)
Name sanitization handles spaces, unicode, >63 chars, collisions
Labels set on both metadata and spec.template.metadata
Test fixtures: single-disk Linux, multi-disk Windows+CDROM, UEFI, multi-NIC, IDE disk

userStories.title: Validation Engine
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pre-flight validation for migration plans. Create internal/validation/validator.go: Validate(ctx, vm, ValidationOptions) returns []Concern. ValidationOptions: NetworkMap, StorageMap, ExistingVMs, TargetNamespace, optional client.Client (nil = skip target-side checks). Concern: Category (Error/Warning/Info), Message. Rules: unmapped storage container (Error), unmapped subnet (Error), GPU present (Warning), Volume Group (Error), NETWORK_FUNCTION_NIC (Error), DIRECT_NIC (Warning: requires SR-IOV), MAC conflict (Warning), namespace missing (Error), IDE disk (Info), StorageClass not found (Error), Multus NAD not found (Error), CDI not installed (Error), KubeVirt not installed (Error).
acceptanceCriteria: Each validation rule implemented and unit tested
Concerns have clear human-readable messages
Error-level concerns block migration, Warning/Info do not
Target-side checks skip gracefully when K8s client is nil
Unit tests for every rule plus combination scenarios

userStories.title: Integration Test Harness + Provider Controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the integration test harness and the Provider controller. Create test/integration/suite_test.go: envtest setup, register VMA CRD schemes, start mock Nutanix server, install VMA CRDs from config/crd/bases/. For KubeVirt/CDI CRDs: register types from Go API packages and create minimal CRD stubs in test/fixtures/crds/ with x-kubernetes-preserve-unknown-fields:true. Add Makefile target test-fixtures. Create internal/controller/provider_controller.go: reconcile NutanixProvider CRs, create Nutanix client from Secret, fetch inventory (ListVMs, ListSubnets, ListClusters, ListStorageContainers per PE), update status, set Connected/InventoryReady conditions, requeue after RefreshInterval, add finalizer. Register controller in cmd/main.go.
acceptanceCriteria: envtest harness starts/stops cleanly
Mock Nutanix server starts in-process for integration tests
Provider controller reconciles: connects, inventories, updates status
Handles bad credentials (sets Error condition)
Re-syncs on RefreshInterval
Finalizer prevents deletion while plans reference it
Integration test: create Provider CR + Secret -> verify status updated
make test-integration passes
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 2a, 3, 4, 6, and 8a. The Provider controller creates its own Nutanix client from the Provider's Secret.

userStories.title: Plan Controller
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Plan controller to validate migration plans. Create internal/controller/plan_controller.go: reconcile MigrationPlan CRs. Resolve Provider reference -> read Secret -> create Nutanix client -> call GetVM(uuid) for each VM. Resolve NetworkMap and StorageMap. Run validation engine. Update per-VM status with concerns. Set phase (Pending -> Validating -> Ready or Error). NOTE: Plan controller creates its OWN Nutanix client from Provider credentials -- does NOT read from Provider status. Register in cmd/main.go.
acceptanceCriteria: Resolves all references (Provider, NetworkMap, StorageMap)
Creates Nutanix client from Provider Secret
Fetches VM details via GetVM for each VM in plan
Runs validation for each VM
Per-VM status with concerns in plan status
Phase transitions: Pending -> Validating -> Ready or Error
Integration test: valid plan -> Ready, GPU VM -> Warning, unmapped storage -> Error

userStories.title: Migration Controller -- Pipeline Framework
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the migration controller skeleton with state machine, phase tracking, concurrency, and error handling. Create internal/controller/migration_controller.go: reconcile Migration CRs. Phase enum: Pending, PreHook, StorePowerState, PowerOff, WaitForPowerOff, CreateSnapshot, ExportDisks, ImportDisks, CreateVM, StartVM, PostHook, Cleanup, Completed, Failed. All phases return PhaseResult{Completed: true} (stubs). MaxInFlight scheduler. Cancellation. Finalizer (vma.nutanix.io/migration-protection). IDEMPOTENCY: each phase checks for existing resources before creating; persist task/snapshot/image UUIDs in VMMigrationStatus. Register in cmd/main.go.
acceptanceCriteria: State machine advances through all phases (stubs succeed immediately)
Per-VM phase tracking in Migration status with persisted resource UUIDs
MaxInFlight limits concurrent VM migrations
Cancellation stops and cleans up individual VMs
Finalizer prevents deletion during running migration
Idempotent: re-reconciling does not create duplicate resources
make build && make test passes

userStories.title: Migration Controller -- Disk Transfer Phases
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the real disk transfer pipeline phases. Replace stubs: PowerOff (client.PowerOffVM, store original power state), CreateSnapshot (client.CreateRecoveryPoint), ExportDisks (client.CreateImageFromDisk for each disk -- if fails, phase fails with error; fallback clone path deferred). Create internal/transfer/manager.go and datavolume.go: CDI DataVolume with HTTP source URL to Prism image download, credential secret (accessKeyId/secretKey format), CA ConfigMap if custom CA, owner reference to Migration CR, volumeMode/accessMode from StorageMap. ImportDisks: create DataVolumes, poll DV status for progress percentage.
acceptanceCriteria: PowerOff calls Nutanix API with task polling
Snapshot created and UUID persisted in status
Image created from snapshot vDisk
CDI DataVolume created with correct HTTP source, credentials, CA config, storage config
Owner reference set on DataVolume -> Migration CR
Credential secret and CA ConfigMap created and cleaned up
Progress percentage tracked from DataVolume status
On failure: restore source VM power state, clean up snapshots/images
Unit tests for transfer manager and datavolume builder
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 13a, Story 5, and Story 9. Fallback export path (clone from recovery point) is deferred to keep scope manageable.

userStories.title: Migration Controller -- VM Creation & Cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the VM creation and cleanup phases wired in, plus the full integration test. Replace CreateVM stub: call builder.Build() with Nutanix VM, maps, options. Create KubeVirt VirtualMachine CR with NO owner reference (outlives Migration). Replace StartVM: set spec.running per targetPowerState. Replace Cleanup: delete temp Nutanix images, snapshots, credential secrets. Replace Failed: clean up DataVolumes, PVCs, snapshots, images, secrets; restore source power state. Create test/integration/migration_test.go: full cold migration against mock+envtest.
acceptanceCriteria: KubeVirt VM created with correct metadata translation from builder
VM running state matches targetPowerState
Cleanup deletes temporary Nutanix resources (images, snapshots, secrets)
Failure cleanup is thorough (no leaked resources)
Integration test: end-to-end cold migration against mock + envtest
make test-integration passes

userStories.title: Migration Controller -- Warm Migration (Experimental)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need warm migration support (experimental -- depends on unverified Nutanix API assumptions). Extend migration controller: warm state machine (BulkCopy via CDI DV -> PrecopyLoop: CreateSnapshot -> ComputeCBT -> TransferDelta -> DeleteOldSnapshot -> repeat at configurable interval -> Cutover: PowerOff -> FinalSnapshot -> FinalDelta -> CreateVM -> StartVM). Create internal/transfer/delta.go: delta transfer pod spec (busybox+dd for MVP). DataVolume created before BulkCopy at full disk size. Wait for CDI DV Succeeded before first precopy. Cutover triggered by spec.cutover timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial bulk copy via CDI DataVolume
Precopy loop creates snapshots and computes CBT deltas
Delta transfer pod spec generated with correct PVC mount and offsets
Precopy repeats at configurable interval (default 30m)
Cutover triggered by spec.cutover timestamp
Progress tracking: precopy rounds, cumulative bytes, last delta size
Integration test with mock CBT data
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 13c, Story 7, Story 8b. EXPERIMENTAL: warm migration depends on unverified Nutanix Image Service Range header support. See PRD FR-5 warning.

userStories.title: Hook System
userStories.description: As a developer, I need pre/post migration hooks. Create internal/controller/hook_runner.go: create K8s Job from Hook CR spec, ConfigMap with migration context (VM details JSON, plan details JSON) mounted at /tmp/hook/, watch Job status, retry on failure (max 3). Wire PreHook and PostHook phases in migration state machine (replace stubs).
acceptanceCriteria: Hook Job created with correct image, service account, deadline
ConfigMap with migration context mounted at /tmp/hook/
Retry on failure (3 attempts before blocking pipeline)
PreHook blocks pipeline until Job succeeds
PostHook runs after CreateVM
Integration test with busybox hook image

userStories.title: kubectl-vma Plugin
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a kubectl plugin for CLI interaction. Create cmd/kubectl-vma/main.go: cobra-based CLI. Subcommands: inventory (read Provider status, table output), plan (read Plan status), migrate (create Migration CR), status (read Migration status with per-VM pipeline progress), cancel (patch Migration cancel list). Use text/tabwriter for table output.
acceptanceCriteria: go build -o kubectl-vma ./cmd/kubectl-vma/ produces binary
inventory command displays VMs with UUID, name, CPU, memory, disk count
status command displays per-VM pipeline phases with progress
migrate command creates a Migration CR
cancel command patches Migration cancel list
Unit tests for output formatting

userStories.title: Observability -- Events, Logging, Metrics
userStories.description: As a developer, I need observability in the operator. Add Kubernetes Events via record.EventRecorder: MigrationStarted, PhaseTransition, MigrationCompleted, MigrationFailed. Add structured logging via logr with fields: migration, vm, phase, duration. Add Prometheus metrics via prometheus/client_golang: vma_migrations_total, vma_migration_duration_seconds, vma_disk_transfer_bytes, vma_active_migrations. Serve /metrics endpoint.
acceptanceCriteria: Events emitted for migration phase transitions
Structured log fields: migration name, VM name/UUID, phase, duration
Prometheus metrics registered and served on /metrics
Integration test verifies events emitted after migration

userStories.title: E2E Test Framework
userStories.description: As a developer, I need E2E tests for real infrastructure validation. Create test/e2e/suite_test.go: Ginkgo suite, reads NUTANIX_PC_URL, NUTANIX_USERNAME, NUTANIX_PASSWORD, KUBEVIRT_KUBECONFIG from env. Skip if NUTANIX_E2E != true. Create test/e2e/cold_migration_test.go: find test VM, create Provider/Maps/Plan/Migration, wait for completion, verify KubeVirt VM exists. Create .github/workflows/e2e.yaml: manual dispatch with secrets. Cleanup after each test.
acceptanceCriteria: Suite skips cleanly without NUTANIX_E2E=true
Cold migration test case (create all CRs, wait, verify VM)
Warm migration test case (gated behind warm migration being implemented)
Cleanup: delete migrated VMs, snapshots, images after each test
GitHub Actions workflow with manual dispatch trigger
userStories.notes: Depends on Story 13c and Story 14 (for warm E2E case).

userStories.title: Documentation & Release
userStories.description: As a developer, I need comprehensive documentation and a release pipeline. Update README.md: overview, architecture diagram, prerequisites, installation (make install && make deploy), quickstart (Provider -> Maps -> Plan -> Migration), CRD reference, configuration, troubleshooting. Create CONTRIBUTING.md: dev setup, testing, PR process. Create .github/workflows/release.yaml: on tag push (v*), build image, push to ghcr.io, create GitHub Release with changelog.
acceptanceCriteria: README covers full user journey from install to migration
CONTRIBUTING.md covers dev setup, testing, PR process
GitHub Actions release workflow: tag -> build -> push image -> create release
CRD reference documentation for all 6 CRDs
Troubleshooting section with common issues
make build && make test still passes
